name: HPV58_E7_oncogene
description: HPV58 E7 oncoprotein-coding region. E7 binds RB1 (and p107/p130) via its LxCxE motif and triggers RB1 proteasomal degradation, releasing E2F transcription factors and forcing S-phase entry. Contains a CR1 region, an LxCxE pocket-binding motif (CR2), and a C-terminal CR3 zinc-finger domain. E7 from high-risk types binds RB with markedly higher affinity than from low-risk types. Together with E6, E7 is the minimal cassette retained on integration and is the principal driver of cervical carcinogenesis. PSV pattern within CR2/CR3 is the basis for type-specific PCR typing.
expected_copy_count: 1 per episomal genome; multiple integrated copies in carcinoma
